title: Glyphotaenium trifurcatum
taxonomy: Glyphotaenium trifurcatum is a genus of lichenized fungi in the family Pyxidiculaceae. It belongs to the phylum Ascomycota, which is a group of fungi characterized by the presence of sac-like structures called ascocarps. Within the phylum Ascomycota, Glyphotaenium trifurcatum is classified as a member of the class Dothideomycetes. Further classification places it within the order Pleosporales, which is a group of fungi known for producing asexual spores. This species is also identified as a member of the family Pyxidiculaceae, which is a family of fungi that are typically lichenized.

intro: Glyphotaenium trifurcatum, also known as the "Trifurcate Liverwort," is a species of liverwort, a type of non-vascular plant that belongs to the division Marchantiophyta. The Trifurcate Liverwort has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. Bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to possess immunomodulatory and antioxidant activities.
